The Permian is home to almost half of all onshore horizontal rigs in the United States. Essentially, it is the place to be for oil and gas production with elevated reserves and low breakeven prices. In a recent article, I showed the chart below. As we can see, the Permian has mostly undeveloped wells, which bodes well for future production. Nonetheless, because of elevated production, more infrastructure is needed in the years ahead, which is one of the reasons why I am also very bullish on the midstream industry.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Axcelis Technologies (ACLS) After Its Strong Five Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Axcelis Technologies is attractively priced right now, the key question is how its current share price stacks up against its underlying fundamentals.
  • The stock closed at US$85.74, with returns of a 0.6% decline over 7 days, a 9.3% decline over 30 days, a 0.4% decline year to date, a 49.4% gain over 1 year, a 34.8% decline over 3 years, and a 135.5% gain over 5 years. This performance can change how you think about both risk and opportunity.
  • Recent news flow around Axcelis has focused on its role within the semiconductor equipment space and how investor expectations compare with peers, which helps set the backdrop for these price swings. Together, these updates give you clues about how sentiment may be shaping the current share price.
  • Simply Wall St currently assigns Axcelis a valuation score of 2 out of 6. Next you will see how traditional approaches like P/E comparisons and discounted cash flow models line up, followed by a more complete way to think about what the stock might be worth.

Axcelis Technologies scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Axcelis Technologies Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today using a required rate of return. It is essentially asking what Axcelis Technologies' future cash generation is worth in today's dollars.

For Axcelis, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $105.9 million, with analysts providing a future free cash flow estimate of $231 million for 2023. Beyond the usual analyst horizon, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows out to 2035, with annual projections such as $96.8 million in 2026 and $100.1 million in 2035, each discounted back to reflect time and risk.

Aggregating these discounted projections gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $35.03 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$85.74, the DCF output suggests the stock is around 144.8% above this model's fair value estimate. This indicates a comparatively rich valuation based on these assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Axcelis Technologies may be overvalued by 144.8%. Discover 49 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Axcelis Technologies.

Approach 2: Axcelis Technologies Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It reflects what the market is willing to pay today based on what it expects from future earnings and how risky those earnings appear to be.

Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tend to justify a lower P/E. Axcelis Technologies currently trades on a P/E of 21.91x. That sits below the broader Semiconductor industry average P/E of about 43.64x and also below the peer average of 59.65x. This may suggest the market is assigning it a lower valuation than many comparables.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio concept goes a step further. It estimates what a company’s P/E might be, given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and key risks. For Axcelis, this Fair Ratio is 18.38x, which is below the current 21.91x. Because Fair Ratio adjusts for company specific fundamentals, it can be more informative than simply lining the stock up against industry or peer averages.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Axcelis Technologies Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives are introduced as a simple way for you to write the story behind your numbers for Axcelis Technologies, linking what you believe about its products, markets, risks and opportunities to a financial forecast, a fair value, and then a clear comparison to the current share price. All of this is done within an easy tool on Simply Wall St’s Community page that is updated when new news or earnings land. One investor might build a Narrative that leans into Axcelis’s leadership in high energy ion implantation, silicon carbide adoption and recurring service revenue and arrive at a higher fair value such as the US$92 central estimate. Another might focus more on China exposure, analyst assumptions for revenue to decrease 2.3% each year, margin compression to 8.0% and earnings of US$66.7 million by 2028 and land closer to or even below the US$85.5 analyst target. This gives you two very different but structured views on whether today’s price of US$75.72 looks attractive or stretched.

data.title: Samsung Electronics Bets Big On AI Chips And New Hardware Growth

data.title: Virbac (ENXTPA:VIRP) Margin Holds Near 10% Keeping Growth Narrative Intact
data.description:
Virbac (ENXTPA:VIRP) has put fresh numbers on the table for FY 2025, reporting second half revenue of €726.4 million and net income of €68.5 million, while the trailing twelve month figures stand at €1.46 billion of revenue and €150.9 million of net income. The company’s revenue has moved from €694.4 million and net income of €50.6 million in 2024 H2 to €726.4 million and €68.5 million in 2025 H2. This comes alongside trailing EPS of €15.88 and sets up a story where investors will be weighing steady profit levels against a slightly softer margin profile.

See our full analysis for Virbac.

With the headline figures in place, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely held narratives about Virbac’s growth, valuation, and risk profile, and where the latest earnings data starts to push back on those views.

See what the community is saying about Virbac

Advertisement

3.9% earnings growth and stable 10.3% margin

  • Over the last 12 months, earnings grew 3.9% with a trailing net profit margin of 10.3%, very close to the prior 10.4% level.
  • Analysts' consensus view links this roughly steady margin to heavier R&D and new facilities, and the data partly supports that tension between spending and profitability:
    • On the one hand, trailing net income of €150.9 million sits above the €150.3 million figure referenced in the longer term narrative. This lines up with the idea that earnings are still holding up while the company funds new projects.
    • On the other hand, the tiny shift in margin from 10.4% to 10.3% shows cost pressure is present. This is consistent with commentary about higher personnel costs and R&D potentially weighing on net margins.

Half year swings versus 3.9% yearly growth

  • Within FY 2025, H1 net income was €82.4 million on €738.3 million of revenue and H2 net income was €68.5 million on €726.4 million of revenue, while over the last year as a whole earnings grew 3.9% and the net margin sat at 10.3%.
  • Consensus narrative suggests facility expansions and M&A in markets like the U.S., China and India underpin the long term growth story, and the mixed half year pattern gives useful context:
    • H2 2025 net income of €68.5 million compared with €50.6 million in H2 2024 sits alongside trailing twelve month revenue of about €1.46b. This is close to the €1.6b revenue level analysts discuss for future years.
    • At the same time, trailing earnings growth of 3.9% is much lower than the roughly 9.9% yearly growth analysts reference for the next few years, so the current run rate leaves some distance between actual results and the more bullish long term expectations.

Bulls argue these steady margins and incremental earnings growth are the first step toward the richer long term story. If that view interests you it is worth seeing how they connect today’s numbers to the bigger picture 🐂 Virbac Bull Case

P/E of 19.6x and DCF fair value of €662.48

  • The shares trade at €353 with a P/E of 19.6x, below peer and industry P/E levels of 21.5x and 21.7x, and compared with a stated DCF fair value of €662.48.
  • Bears focus on growth constraints, and the current metrics both support and challenge that cautious angle:
    • Forecast earnings growth of about 9.9% per year and revenue growth of around 5.3% per year are below the cited French market averages. This lines up with concerns about reliance on slower regions and missing exposure to some higher growth product areas.
    • However, the roughly 46.7% gap between the share price and DCF fair value, alongside a P/E that sits below peer and industry averages, means the stock is not priced at a premium despite those lower forecast growth rates.

Skeptics often point to that slower forecast growth. If you want to see how they build the cautious case around it, check out the detailed bear side of the story 🐻 Virbac Bear Case

Next Steps

If this mix of cautious and optimistic signals feels finely balanced, do not wait for consensus to settle. Instead, weigh the evidence yourself and see how the 3 key rewards

data.title: Intel’s AI Role Deepens With DGX Rubin And Versa Edge Wins
data.description:
• Intel's new Xeon 6 processors are confirmed to power NVIDIA's DGX Rubin NVL8 AI system, signaling a key role in advanced AI computing hardware.
  • Intel announced a collaboration with Versa to apply AI-driven security and analytics at the Intelligent Edge, focusing on real world deployment of AI workloads.
  • These partnerships reflect tangible design wins for Intel in AI infrastructure and edge security, moving beyond prior alliance announcements.

For investors tracking NasdaqGS:INTC, the new design wins arrive with the stock at $45.03, with a 1 year return of 86.7% and a 3 year return of 64.0%. The 5 year return of 20.2% decline shows that longer term holders have had a different experience than those who entered more recently, which can influence how you frame these latest developments.

The Xeon 6 role in NVIDIA's DGX Rubin NVL8 and the Versa edge security collaboration highlight where Intel is currently getting real AI traction, rather than just focusing on future roadmaps. As you assess Intel's position in accelerated computing and AI infrastructure, these concrete deployments provide fresh information to weigh alongside existing views on its product strategy and competitive footing.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Intel by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Intel.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 2 things going right for Intel that every investor should see.

For Intel, sitting inside NVIDIA’s DGX Rubin NVL8 AI system with Xeon 6 while also being selected by Versa for AI-driven security at the Intelligent Edge points to a clear role in real deployments, not just reference designs. In data centers, Intel’s CPUs are effectively the control layer around GPU clusters, so design wins in a flagship NVIDIA system help keep Intel present in high end AI infrastructure where GPUs from NVIDIA, AMD or custom ASICs tend to dominate headlines. At the edge, the Versa partnership ties Xeon 6 to secure networking and analytics where low latency and data locality matter, a segment where Intel competes with players like AMD and Qualcomm.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Intel Narrative

  • The narrative highlights Intel’s push into AI workloads and foundry services, and these wins with NVIDIA and Versa line up with that focus by showing CPUs designed into both data center and edge AI systems.
  • The narrative also flags execution complexity and capacity constraints, and these new commitments could stretch engineering and supply if Intel struggles to deliver consistent performance, software support or volume.
  • The specific role of Xeon 6 in DGX Rubin NVL8 and in Versa’s Universal SASE platform is not fully reflected in the narrative summary, so the depth of this CPU centric AI position may be underappreciated.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Intel to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Intel was the only top 10 chipmaker to see revenue fall in 2025, so relying too heavily on AI partnerships to offset broader business pressure could leave expectations ahead of delivery.
  • ⚠️ Foundry losses of US$10.3b in 2025 and a path to only break even margins by 2027 mean capital and execution risk stay high even as Intel lines up more AI and edge wins.
  • 🎁 Embedding Xeon 6 in NVIDIA’s DGX Rubin NVL8 and in Versa’s Intelligent Edge architecture helps keep Intel relevant in high value AI infrastructure beside rivals such as NVIDIA, AMD and Taiwan Semiconductor.
  • 🎁 These contracts add to a growing set of AI and 6G collaborations with Ericsson, Infosys and Viettel High Tech, giving Intel multiple channels where AI centric demand could support its longer term reset.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth tracking how often NVIDIA and Versa reference Intel in future product updates, whether Xeon 6 gains further AI design wins, and how Intel starts to quantify AI related revenue against wider group sales that recently declined. It also helps to watch commentary from large customers around CPU versus GPU mix for AI workloads, and any updates from Intel on foundry profitability targets and supply constraints, to see if growing AI exposure is matched by improving financial performance.

United already competes across domestic and international routes, and premium cabins have become a focus area across global airlines as travelers pay closer attention to comfort and privacy on longer flights.

The rollout of Polaris Studio suites on the 787-9 and the parallel moves at Washington Dulles and Chicago O'Hare provide a concrete view of where United is choosing to invest and where it is constrained. How the company executes on these aircraft upgrades and manages hub capacity rules could influence future route planning, customer mix, and overall cost structure.
